title: Setting date push for the same number of days as in the "dependent on" task
excerpt:
Hi guys, 
I’ve just made a board with a workflow of one process in our company. The thing is, some of the tasks are dependent on the others’ due dates. I’ve tried making a dependency and setting an automation for a date …

posts.author: hannakmiec (Hanna Kmiec)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,
I’ve just made a board with a workflow of one process in our company. The thing is, some of the tasks are dependent on the others’ due dates. I’ve tried making a dependency and setting an automation for a date change, but I haven’t found an option of pushing one task due date for the same number of days as the previous task in the workflow. Is it even possible?
For example, for my first task, Task 1, I set the date for the 5th of August and for the second task my standard time is 3 days after Task 2, so I put August, 8th. Two weeks after task 2 i have task 3 set. Now I had to move the task 1 to the 10th of August and I’d like the deadline for task 2 and task 3 to be set accordingly (so +5 days on both tasks - and it would be perfect to set it on Workdays, if possible). Do you know if I can create such an automation?

Thank you so much!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@hannakmiec

This automation does exactly that:

posts.author: leonidas (Leonidas)
posts.cookedHtml: I am a pro user and I cannot find this automation, not create it from scratch. Has it been removed?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@leonidas

Yes, this has been removed as it is no longer needed due to the recent changes in how dependencies are handled. To set the dependencies to work in the same way that the automation would have made it work, choose “Strict” from the settings area of the dependency column.
